title: [Improvement] Work with gems requiring build steps
author: skull-squadron
body:
nokogiri, ffi, and such require build steps.

It Would Be Nice™ to be able to run a shell command in the context of the gem about to be built.

This would allow rake or something to prepare native native extensions as maintainers would built it.

title: "undefined method `exists?' for File:Class" in Ruby v3
author: andrzej-stencel
body:
Hi @rdp and thanks for this great gem. We have been using it as part of our open source collection software at @SumoLogic for some time now: https://github.com/SumoLogic/sumologic-kubernetes-fluentd/blob/v1.14.6-sumo-5/Dockerfile#L32.

Now we want to start using Ruby v3 and I'm getting the `undefined method exists? for File:Class`, which has apparently just been [fixed](https://github.com/rdp/specific_install/pull/57). I think we need a new release of the gem to use it. Can you make it please?

Again, thanks for your work on this gem. Let me know if I can help in any way.

title: new method for verifying git is not working
author: hagaivcita
body:
the new method for checking if `git` binaries are installed is not working in every situations since ruby's `system` call does not find shell builtins (ruby 2.7.0).

while using this Gem in my docker environment using `FROM ubuntu:18.04` and `ruby 2.7.0` the invocation of `system("type -a git")` returns `nil`, specifying the command `type` was not found even though it is a shell builtin.

title: Fails to install gem if it has prerelease gems in its dependencies.
author: shkrt
body:
For example:
```
$ gem specific_install 'rails/rails'
```
or 
```
$ gem specific_install 'https://github.com/rails/rails.git'
```
or
```
$ gem specific_install -l 'https://github.com/rails/rails.git'
```
results in:
```
$ ERROR:  While executing gem ... (Gem::UnsatisfiableDependencyError)
    Unable to resolve dependency: 'rails (= 5.2.0.alpha)' requires 'railties (= 5.2.0.alpha)'
```
and if we start to install dependencies one-by-one, we also got stuck:
```
$ gem specific_install 'https://github.com/rails/rails.git' -d activesupport`  # -Success
$ gem list activesupport 
$ activesupport (5.2.0.alpha, 5.1.4)
$ gem specific_install 'https://github.com/rails/rails.git' -d actionview
$ ERROR:  While executing gem ... (Gem::DependencyError)
    Unresolved dependency found during sorting - activesupport (>= 4.2.0) (requested by rails-dom-testing-2.0.3)
```
This is caused by the presence of `prerelease` gems in gem's dependencies. If we initialize `Gem::DependencyInstaller` with `prerelease:true`, then at least `gem specific_install 'rails/rails' -d actionview ` seems to work fine. So, I think it would be convenient to add some command line flag, that will set `prerelease` option to `true`, if provided.

title: support  --user-install option
author: jimkont
body:
This is a feature request
It would be nice to add a `--user-install` option as defined in `gem install xyz --user-install`

title: Integration into official rubygems?
author: ghost
body:
Hey there,

Feel free to close this issue at any time.

I had a look at the functionality and it seems to be a useful addition.

Bundler allows this as far as I know already whereas rubygem does
not allow github-based installation, I think (from the gem commandline).

So I was wondering, have you asked the rubygem core team whether
they would like to integrate it; and of course, before that, would you
even want to have this functionality included into rubygems itself?

title: Fix option descriptions in `--help` output
author: bgerstle
body:
Redundantly and inaccurately uses `LOCATION` to describe all the options:

```
  Options:
    -l, --location LOCATION          LOCATION like http://github.com/rdp/ruby_tutorials_core or git://github.com/rdp/ruby_tutorials_core.git or http://host/gem_name.gem
BRANCH (optional) like beta, or new-feature
DIRECTORY (optional) This will change the directory in the downloaded source directory before building the gem.
    -b, --branch LOCATION            LOCATION like http://github.com/rdp/ruby_tutorials_core or git://github.com/rdp/ruby_tutorials_core.git or http://host/gem_name.gem
BRANCH (optional) like beta, or new-feature
DIRECTORY (optional) This will change the directory in the downloaded source directory before building the gem.
    -d, --directory DIRECTORY        LOCATION like http://github.com/rdp/ruby_tutorials_core or git://github.com/rdp/ruby_tutorials_core.git or http://host/gem_name.gem
BRANCH (optional) like beta, or new-feature
DIRECTORY (optional) This will change the directory in the downloaded source directory before building the gem.
```

title: Fails with sudo because of ssh key error
author: jdrago999
body:
Hi -

When running as a non-root user I can clone the repo, but cannot install the gem.

Under sudo I can install the gem, but cloning the repo fails because of ssh key error.

To solve this problem I propose the following:
- add an additional option `--sudo` which would cause the 'install' part to be done via sudo (on supporting platforms).

I'd rather this functionality become part of the 'specific_install' gem than create a separate project due to this one small feature.

Thoughts?

title: git must be installed before using this plugin
author: mikeweinberg
body:
I started on a new host that did not have git installed- I kept getting the non-specific errors below but installing git fixed the problem. I'm hoping filing this issue saves someone some time wondering why this gem doesn't work for them. 

```
[username@host ~]$ gem specific_install -l https://github.com/username/repository.git -b master
git installing from https://github.com/username/repository.git

attempting rake gemspec...

```

title: Flag to also clone submodules
author: dacamp
body:
For some gems, such as [libgit2/rugged](https://github.com/libgit2/rugged) submodules are required at compile time.

```
gem specific_install https://github.com/libgit2/rugged.git
git installing from https://github.com/libgit2/rugged.git
Cloning into '/var/folders/00/1t0j8000h01000cxqpysvccm007825/T/d20141202-2826-1i9dru5'...
remote: Counting objects: 10171, done.
remote: Total 10171 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0)
Receiving objects: 100% (10171/10171), 18.65 MiB | 4.13 MiB/s, done.
Resolving deltas: 100% (5399/5399), done.
Checking connectivity... done.
WARNING:  open-ended dependency on rake-compiler (>= 0.9.0, development) is not recommended
  if rake-compiler is semantically versioned, use:
    add_development_dependency 'rake-compiler', '~> 0.9', '>= 0.9.0'
WARNING:  open-ended dependency on pry (>= 0, development) is not recommended
  if pry is semantically versioned, use:
    add_development_dependency 'pry', '~> 0'
WARNING:  pessimistic dependency on minitest (~> 3.0.0, development) may be overly strict
  if minitest is semantically versioned, use:
    add_development_dependency 'minitest', '~> 3.0', '>= 3.0.0'
WARNING:  See http://guides.rubygems.org/specification-reference/ for help
  Successfully built RubyGem
  Name: rugged
  Version: 0.22.0b3
  File: rugged-0.22.0b3.gem
Building native extensions.  This could take a while...
ERROR:  While executing gem ... (Gem::Ext::BuildError)
    ERROR: Failed to build gem native extension.

    /Users/dacamp/.rvm/rubies/ruby-2.0.0-p481/bin/ruby extconf.rb
checking for gmake... no
checking for make... yes
checking for cmake... yes
checking for pkg-config... yes
*** extconf.rb failed ***
Could not create Makefile due to some reason, probably lack of necessary
libraries and/or headers.  Check the mkmf.log file for more details.  You may
need configuration options.

Provided configuration options:
    --with-opt-dir
    --without-opt-dir
    --with-opt-include
    --without-opt-include=${opt-dir}/include
    --with-opt-lib
    --without-opt-lib=${opt-dir}/lib
    --with-make-prog
    --without-make-prog
    --srcdir=.
    --curdir
    --ruby=/Users/dacamp/.rvm/rubies/ruby-2.0.0-p481/bin/ruby
    --use-system-libraries
extconf.rb:46:in `chdir': No such file or directory - /Users/dacamp/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.0.0-p481/gems/rugged-0.22.0b3/ext/rugged/../../vendor/libgit2 (Errno::ENOENT)
    from extconf.rb:46:in `<main>'

extconf failed, exit code 1
```

This is because rugged has a submodule in vendor/libgit2.  It would be helpful if there was a flag such as `--recursive`, that would change your `git clone` commands to `git clone --recursive` inside of specific_install_command.rb.

Thanks!

title: ignoring ~/.gemrc options
author: jpcirrus
body:
specific_install 0.2.11, ruby 2.0.0, os x 10.9.4:

When installing with say `sudo gem specific_install -l https://github.com/hisashim/docdiff.git` the options specified in the `~/.gemrc` are ignored. But when using `sudo gem install docdiff` the options in `~/.gemrc` are applied.
